title: Lead-Based Paint Poisoning Prevention Act
summary: Created a federal framework for the detection, treatment, and elimination of lead-based paint poisoning, especially in federally assisted housing and among children facing elevated exposure risk.
outcome_summary: Established the first major federal lead-poisoning law, addressing a public-health hazard concentrated in older and underinvested housing stock where Black children and families have long faced disproportionate exposure.
impact_notes: Important because toxic housing conditions are part of the public-health and housing inequality story, not just an environmental regulation issue.

scores.notes: A foundational lead-poisoning intervention with strong relevance to racialized housing and child-health disparities.

sources.source_title: H.R.19172 - Lead-Based Paint Poisoning Prevention Act
sources.notes: Official bill page and action history confirming enactment as Public Law 91-695.

sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
sources.notes: CDC overview showing the continuing scale of lead-paint hazards in older housing and the disproportionate exposure risk for children.

metrics.metric_name: Housing units with lead-based paint hazards
metrics.methodology_note: CDC stated in March 2025 that approximately 29 million housing units still have lead-based paint hazards.

metrics.metric_name: Housing units with lead-based paint hazards that are home to young children
metrics.methodology_note: CDC stated in March 2025 that about 2.6 million housing units with lead-based paint hazards are home to young children.

title: Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990
summary: Prohibited disability discrimination across employment, public services, public accommodations, telecommunications, and other major areas of civic and economic life.
outcome_summary: Created the central federal civil-rights framework for disability access and anti-discrimination, with important implications for Black disabled Americans who face overlapping barriers in employment, transportation, schooling, housing, and public services.
impact_notes: Important not only as disability law, but as an equity law affecting Black Americans living at the intersection of racial and disability discrimination.

scores.notes: A landmark civil-rights law with broad consequences for access, participation, and anti-discrimination enforcement.

sources.source_title: H.R.2273 - Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990
sources.notes: Congress.gov bill record and CRS summary describing the ADA's core prohibitions and protections.
DOJ ADA portal describing the law's scope and the opportunities it is meant to guarantee.

title: Executive Order 12898
summary: Directed federal agencies to identify and address disproportionately high and adverse human health or environmental effects of their actions on minority and low-income populations and established an interagency environmental justice framework.
outcome_summary: Placed environmental justice into federal administrative practice and linked environmental decision-making to civil-rights obligations, though implementation has remained uneven and heavily dependent on agency follow-through.
impact_notes: A foundational environmental-justice action because it formally recognized that minority communities, including Black communities, bear disproportionate environmental and health harms.

scores.notes: A major federal recognition of environmental racism and public-health inequity, though not self-executing.

sources.source_title: Summary of Executive Order 12898 - Federal Actions to Address Environmental Justice in Minority Populations and Low-Income Populations
sources.notes: EPA summary of the executive order's purpose, directives, and interagency framework.
EPA explanation of how environmental justice intersects with Title VI's race-discrimination rules in federally funded programs.

title: Olmstead v. L. C.
summary: The Supreme Court held that unjustified institutional segregation of people with disabilities can violate Title II of the ADA and that states must provide services in the most integrated appropriate setting when reasonably possible.
outcome_summary: Became the leading integration mandate in disability civil-rights law, expanding the legal basis for community-based services over unnecessary institutionalization, with important implications for Black disabled Americans disproportionately exposed to segregated or under-resourced public systems.
impact_notes: Important because disability segregation and institutionalization are also racial-equity issues when access to community care and public services is unequal.

scores.notes: A landmark disability-integration ruling with broad significance for equitable access to public systems and community living.

sources.notes: Primary case text and syllabus for the landmark ADA integration decision.
